mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone-mediated potassium excretion; potassium supplementation adds exogenous potassium load.
recommendation: Avoid routine potassium supplementation unless there is a clear indication and potassium/renal function are monitored.
monitoring: ECG if symptomatic or severe hyperkalemia suspected
source: Local Interaction Rule: ACEI + potassium v1

risk: Acute kidney injury, reduced antihypertensive effect, possible hyperkalemia
mechanism: NSAIDs constrict the afferent renal arteriole while ACE inhibitors dilate the efferent arteriole, reducing glomerular filtration pressure in susceptible patients.
recommendation: Use the shortest NSAID duration if unavoidable, avoid in high-risk renal/dehydrated patients, and check renal function and potassium when clinically relevant.
source: Local Interaction Rule: ACEI + NSAID v1

mechanism: ACE inhibitor/ARB + diuretic + NSAID can reduce renal perfusion and filtration through combined hemodynamic effects.
recommendation: Avoid the combination when possible. If unavoidable, ensure hydration, baseline renal function/electrolytes, short NSAID duration, and follow-up labs.
source: Local Clinical Rule: Triple whammy v1

mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits warfarin metabolism, increasing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Requires close INR monitoring and prescriber-led warfarin dose adjustment. Counsel for bleeding symptoms.
source: Local Interaction Rule: warfarin + amiodarone v1

mechanism: Alpha-1 blockade and PDE5 inhibition both lower vascular tone and can have additive blood-pressure lowering effects.
recommendation: Avoid unsafe self-combination. If prescribed together, use cautious timing/dosing and counsel about dizziness, syncope, and standing slowly.
source: Local Interaction Rule: alpha-blocker + PDE5 inhibitor v1

mechanism: ARBs reduce aldosterone-mediated potassium excretion; potassium supplements increase potassium load.
recommendation: Avoid routine potassium supplementation unless indicated and monitored.
source: Local Interaction Rule: ARB + potassium v1

risk: Bleeding risk due to combined anticoagulant and antiplatelet effect
mechanism: Warfarin anticoagulation plus aspirin platelet inhibition increases bleeding risk.
recommendation: Only combine when there is a clear prescriber-approved indication. Counsel for bleeding and monitor closely.
source: Local Interaction Rule: warfarin + aspirin v1

mechanism: ARB efferent arteriolar dilation plus NSAID afferent arteriolar constriction plus diuretic-related volume depletion can sharply reduce glomerular filtration pressure, especially during diarrhea/poor intake or CKD.
recommendation: Avoid the combination when possible. In AKI/dehydration/hypotension, urgently flag for clinician review to hold nephrotoxic/volume-depleting contributors and monitor renal function/electrolytes.
source: Local Clinical Rule: ARB + NSAID + diuretic triple whammy v1

mechanism: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4 and P-gp, reducing colchicine metabolism/efflux and increasing systemic exposure; renal impairment further reduces clearance.
recommendation: Avoid the combination, especially in renal or hepatic impairment. Urgently flag for prescriber review to stop/replace the interacting drug(s) and assess for toxicity.
source: Local Interaction Rule: colchicine + clarithromycin v1

risk: Increased statin exposure with myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk
mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 and can increase atorvastatin concentrations; dehydration/AKI and colchicine exposure can further amplify myotoxicity risk.
recommendation: Consider temporary statin hold or alternative antibiotic/statin strategy under prescriber direction; assess CK and muscle symptoms when clinically indicated.
source: Local Interaction Rule: clarithromycin + atorvastatin v1

mechanism: Tramadol inhibits serotonin/norepinephrine reuptake and lowers seizure threshold; concurrent SSRI/SNRI therapy increases serotonergic tone.
recommendation: Flag urgently for prescriber review; avoid tramadol with multiple serotonergic antidepressants and assess for serotonin toxicity.
source: Local Interaction Rule: tramadol + serotonergic antidepressants v1

risk: Additive QT prolongation and torsades risk
mechanism: Both can delay cardiac repolarization through hERG/IKr effects; hypokalemia and hypomagnesemia amplify torsades risk.
recommendation: Avoid or hold QT-prolonging contributors in patients with QTc >500 ms and correct electrolytes under clinician direction.
source: Local Interaction Rule: amitriptyline + ondansetron QT risk v1

mechanism: SSRIs/SNRIs can increase ADH release or renal sensitivity to ADH, leading to dilutional hyponatremia; risk is higher in older adults and with thiazides.
recommendation: Check serum sodium and evaluate SIADH/hypovolemia in confused or seizure-prone patients; flag antidepressant regimen for review.
source: Local Interaction Rule: SSRI/SNRI hyponatremia v1

risk: Occult bleeding after trauma/fall, especially with hemoglobin drop
mechanism: Factor Xa inhibition reduces clot formation; trauma can cause intracranial, retroperitoneal, GI, or soft-tissue bleeding without obvious external bleeding.
recommendation: In an anticoagulated patient with recent fall plus new confusion or hemoglobin drop, flag for urgent bleeding assessment and imaging/labs per local protocol.
monitoring: head CT if head injury or confusion
source: Local Safety Rule: apixaban fall and occult bleeding v1
